This surge follows the outbreak of war in the Middle East, triggered by US and Israeli military strikes on Iran, prompting Iranian retaliatory attacks on Gulf nations including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and others. These attacks have severely disrupted oil and natural gas production and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies where over 80 tankers normally pass daily. Shipping has slowed dramatically due to threats and attacks, causing wholesale fuel prices in the UK to jump significantly—diesel by 7 pence per liter and petrol by 2 pence initially, with further increases expected. The Petrol Retailers' Association (PRA) emphasized that retailers are passing on genuine wholesale cost increases rather than profiteering, advising drivers to compare prices locally. Brent crude oil rose over 5% to ar

item.title: Maxim Shkret's Colorful and Expressive Animal Illustrations
item.description: Digital artist Maxim Shkret has created a captivating collection of animal portraits characterized by bold colors, dynamic compositions, and a unique futuristic style. His work spans a wide range of creatures, from polar bears to hummingbirds, and even imaginative five-pointed unicorns. Each portrait is meticulously crafted without the use of AI, emphasizing Shkret's manual artistic skill and attention to detail. The illustrations stand out for their vibrant color combinations and dramatic yet inviting aesthetic, capturing both the energy and personality of the animals. Shkret's approach transforms traditional animal portraiture into a maximalist, expressive experience, inviting viewers to engage with wildlife in a visually stimulating way. The artist shares his work on platforms like Instagram, where the vivid and playful style has garnered significant attention from art enthusiasts. Moss and Fog highlights Shkret's ability to blend artistry and imagination, offering a fresh perspecti

item.title: New Analysis Shows Tyrannosaurus rex Grew Over Four Decades
item.description: A new study challenges previous assumptions about the growth of Tyrannosaurus rex, suggesting that this iconic dinosaur took approximately 40 years to reach its full size, rather than the earlier estimate of 25 years. By analyzing growth rings in the leg bones of 17 tyrannosaur specimens, researchers used advanced statistical modeling and specialized lighting techniques to detect previously overlooked growth markers. This comprehensive approach allowed them to reconstruct a more detailed life history of T. rex, showing a prolonged growth period rather than rapid maturation. The study also raises questions about the classification of some fossils, suggesting that certain specimens, including the well-known 'Jane' and 'Petey,' might represent different species or subspecies within the Tyrannosaurus complex. The findings imply that T. rex's extended development may have allowed juveniles to occupy different ecological roles, contributing to the species' dominance as apex predators at the

item.title: Countries Affected by the Ongoing Conflict Involving Iran
item.description: Since February 28, 2026, a series of military strikes by the United States and Israel against Iran has triggered a regional escalation affecting over a dozen Middle Eastern countries. Iran has retaliated with drone and missile attacks targeting Israel, US embassies, military bases, and key infrastructure across neighboring nations. Casualties have exceeded a thousand in the region, with damage to schools, hospitals, airports, ports, luxury hotels, and military facilities. Countries directly impacted include Israel, Iran, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Cyprus, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Turkey, and the United Arab Emirates. The conflict has disrupted global supply chains, particularly in oil, gas, and fertilizer production, and halted shipping along the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Some countries, including the US, UK, Germany, Ireland, and Italy, have begun evacuating citizens due to safety concerns. Although the Trump administration justifies the military op

item.title: Ocean Patterns Limit Global Drought Spread and Protect Food Security
item.description: Researchers at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (IITGN), collaborating with international partners, have found that ocean temperature patterns significantly influence the spread of droughts across the globe. Analyzing over a century of climate data (1901–2020), the team discovered that synchronized droughts rarely affect more than 1.8%–6.5% of global land at the same time, which is much lower than previous estimates suggesting up to one-sixth of the planet could face simultaneous drought. This limited spread is largely driven by ocean cycles such as El Niño and La Niña, which create a patchwork of regional drought conditions rather than a single, planet-wide dry spell. The study identifies recurring 'drought hubs' including Australia, South America, southern Africa, and parts of North America. While moderate droughts can increase crop failure risks—sometimes exceeding 40% for maize and soybeans—natural oceanic variability helps mitigate the risk of simultaneous global cro

item.title: Nida Allam Concedes Narrow Loss to Valerie Foushee in North Carolina Democratic Primary
item.description: Durham County Commissioner Nida Allam conceded the Democratic primary for North Carolina's 4th Congressional District after a closely contested race against incumbent Rep. Valerie Foushee. Allam, 32, emphasized her progressive platform, which included labeling the Gaza conflict as genocide, abolishing ICE, and ending U.S. involvement in wars abroad. She criticized the Democratic establishment for aligning with corporate Super PACs and warmongering lobbies and highlighted that her campaign drove a significant increase in voter turnout, particularly among young voters. Foushee, who received endorsements from Gov. Josh Stein and former Gov. Roy Cooper, narrowly won with 49.2% of the vote compared to Allam's 48.2%, according to NBC News. Despite the loss, Allam framed the campaign as a milestone for representation, especially for young, Muslim, immigrant women in politics. Her campaign was supported by progressive groups such as Justice Democrats, Leaders We Deserve, Sunrise Movement, Indi

item.title: GLP-1 medications may help reduce heart tissue damage after heart attacks, study finds
item.description: A recent study published in Nature Communications by the University of Bristol and University College London suggests that GLP-1 drugs, such as semaglutide, commonly used for type 2 diabetes and obesity, may reduce the risk of serious heart complications after heart attacks. The research identifies a brain-gut-heart signaling pathway that allows these medications to open potassium channels in pericytes, tiny cells that control the dilation of microscopic blood vessels in the heart. This process can improve blood flow to heart tissue that remains blocked after standard emergency treatments, a condition known as 'no-reflow,' which affects nearly half of heart attack patients and significantly increases the risk of heart failure and death within a year. While animal models and cellular imaging demonstrated this mechanism, the study notes that clinical trials are required to confirm if the same benefits occur in humans. Additionally, the research focuses on the drugs' immediate protective

item.title: Kristi Noem Removed as Homeland Security Secretary Amid Controversial Policies
item.description: Kristi Noem has been removed from her role as Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security after a tenure marked by intense scrutiny over aggressive immigration enforcement and controversial federal operations. President Donald Trump announced her replacement by Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma, a loyal ally and immigration hardliner, while Noem will transition to a newly created role as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas. During her time at DHS, Noem prioritized mass arrests and deportations, clashing with other administration officials over enforcement strategies. Her leadership has been criticized for policies allowing ICE to enter private homes without warrants, the deaths of U.S. citizens during federal operations in Minneapolis, and mass detention camps. Additionally, her micromanagement of FEMA contracts caused significant backlogs, affecting disaster response capabilities. Her tenure also included a $38 billion plan to expand detention facilities nationwide. A

item.title: Kaitlan Collins Defends CNN Coverage of Fallen U.S. Troops After White House Clash
item.description: CNN correspondent Kaitlan Collins responded to a tense exchange with White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt over coverage of the U.S. military's casualties in Iran. The conflict arose after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth criticized news outlets, accusing them of attempting to make President Donald Trump look bad. Collins clarified on her show 'The Source' that the network's reporting focuses on honoring the Americans who lost their lives, not on the president or the network itself. She listed the names of the fallen service members, emphasizing their sacrifice. During the briefing, Leavitt insisted that the media should highlight the successes of Operation Epic Fury and criticized Collins and CNN for allegedly negative coverage of President Trump. Collins maintained that her reporting is intended to honor the troops and their families, highlighting the human cost of the conflict. The exchange reflects ongoing tension between the administration and the press over how military oper

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4 With Integrated Excel and Google Sheets Functionality
item.description: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.4, an upgraded version of its ChatGPT model, designed to improve efficiency, accuracy, and cost-effectiveness for workplace tasks. This new model allows users to work directly within Excel and Google Sheets, enabling faster and more reliable creation of files and problem-solving. According to OpenAI, GPT-5.4 outperforms human office workers on 83% of the tasks evaluated through GDPval, a benchmark measuring real-world performance across 44 different occupations. The model is optimized to use fewer tokens, which helps deliver faster responses while reducing operational costs. Alongside GPT-5.4, OpenAI launched OpenAI for Financial Services, a suite of tools that includes spreadsheet integration and specialized apps and skills within ChatGPT. The initiative is supported by partners such as FactSet, MSCI, Third Bridge, and Moody's, aiming to enhance productivity and analytical capabilities in finance and other office workflows. OpenAI emphasizes that this versi

item.title: Middle East Conflict Threatens Fertilizer Supply and US Farming
item.description: The US attack on Iran has disrupted global supply chains beyond oil, with significant consequences for American agriculture. Fertilizer markets, crucial for spring planting, are under strain as key exporters in the Middle East reduce production. Nitrogen fertilizers, particularly urea and ammonia, are at risk due to halted operations in Qatar and Iran, which together account for a large portion of global output. Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Egypt, and Israel also play major roles in phosphate and potash exports. The blockage of the Strait of Hormuz has slowed shipping and intensified supply challenges. Prices for fertilizers in the US have already risen, and farmers may face shortages during a critical planting window. Unlike oil, there is no strategic reserve for fertilizers in the US, leaving farmers vulnerable. Those cultivating large row crops like corn, soybeans, wheat, and cotton may be forced to ration fertilizer or adjust planting strategies. Compounded by the previous trade war with

item.title: Britney Spears arrested on suspicion of DUI in California, released after overnight custody
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears was arrested by California highway patrol officers on Wednesday evening on suspicion of driving under the influence. She was detained at 9:28 PM and released the next morning at 6:07 AM, according to the Ventura County Sheriff's Office. Spears, who is 5ft 5ins tall and weighs 125lbs, is scheduled to appear in court on May 4. Her representative described the arrest as 'completely inexcusable' but emphasized it could mark a turning point for changes in her personal life. Spears’ team also stated that she will take the necessary steps to comply with the law and receive support during this challenging period, with her children and loved ones involved in planning her well-being. Spears previously gained worldwide attention during her 2021 legal battle to end her conservatorship, which had placed her father in control of her personal and financial affairs. While she has been musically inactive for over a decade, her classic hits such as 'Toxic' and '...Baby One More T

item.title: Stocks Contributing Most to Dow Jones Decline Today
item.description: In today's stock market update from The Wall Street Journal's live coverage on March 5, 2026, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced downward pressure from several key components. The article highlights specific stocks identified as the primary drags on the index during trading sessions. Typically, in such live market reports, underperforming blue-chip companies like those in financials, industrials, or consumer sectors lead the declines, often due to earnings disappointments, macroeconomic concerns, or sector-specific news. For instance, major constituents such as Boeing, Goldman Sachs, or UnitedHealth might be cited for significant point losses, amplified by their heavy weighting in the price-weighted Dow. The report would detail the point contributions to the overall drop, e.g., if Boeing fell 2% contributing -150 points or similar. Broader context includes S&P 500 and Nasdaq performances, potentially mixed amid volatility from Federal Reserve signals, tariff discussions under

item.title: UK Competition Watchdog Launches Investigation into Rising Private Dental Costs
item.description: The UK's £8 billion private dentistry sector is set to undergo an official investigation after reports of substantial price increases across services. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA), responsible for monitoring fair market practices, has expressed concern over these rising costs, which could have serious implications for patients relying on private dental care. While public dental services remain available, many individuals opt for private care to avoid long waiting times or to access specialized treatments. However, the recent price surges have raised questions about transparency, potential anti-competitive practices, and affordability. Analysts suggest that this could affect thousands of patients nationwide, particularly those seeking routine procedures such as check-ups, fillings, or cosmetic dental work. The CMA's investigation aims to understand whether these increases reflect genuine operational costs or if certain practices are exploiting market positions. The move i

item.title: U.S. Stocks Rebound Amid Economic Data and Easing Oil Concerns
item.description: U.S. stock markets closed higher on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbing 238.14 points (0.49%) to 48,739.41, ending a three-day losing streak. The S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,869.50, and the Nasdaq Composite advanced 1.29% to 22,807.48. Technology stocks, particularly in semiconductors, led the gains with Micron Technology and Advanced Micro Devices jumping more than 5%, while Broadcom and Nvidia also posted solid gains. Positive economic data contributed to investor optimism. ADP reported that private-sector employment exceeded expectations in February, and the ISM nonmanufacturing index rose to 56.1, signaling robust growth in the services sector with easing inflation pressures. Meanwhile, oil prices eased after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and President Donald Trump announced measures to ensure safe maritime trade through the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened tensions with Iran. Other market movers included Moderna, Ross Stores, Tesla, and Oracle,

item.title: Music Streaming CEO Develops Open-Source Platform to Track Global Conflicts in Real Time
item.description: Elie Habib, CEO of Anghami, a major Middle Eastern music streaming platform, created World Monitor, an open-source dashboard designed to track global conflicts in real time. Initially a side project, Habib built the platform to make sense of the increasing complexity of international news, integrating over 100 data streams including military aircraft positions, maritime traffic, satellite detections, and internet outages. Unlike traditional news aggregators, World Monitor uses a multi-tiered source verification system combined with a convergence algorithm that highlights credible events based on multiple simultaneous signals. The platform rapidly gained traction when U.S. and Israeli strikes in Iran disrupted air and maritime traffic, drawing millions of users worldwide. Habib continues to develop the system, adding new features such as instant alerts, Telegram intelligence retrieval, and risk advisories for embassies. While not intended as a business, World Monitor is evolving toward

item.title: Former CIA Director Warns Iran's Attacks on Civilians Could Escalate Regional Conflict
item.description: Former CIA Director General David Petraeus warned that Iran has made a strategic mistake by targeting civilians in the Middle East, in addition to attacking US and allied military bases. Speaking to Sky News presenter Yalda Hakim, Petraeus highlighted that Iran's strikes have hit airports, seaports, and gas and oil loading stations across Gulf states, signaling an escalation that could involve previously neutral countries. He noted a drone attack launched by Hezbollah from southern Lebanon against a UK base in Cyprus, which could draw more nations into the conflict. Petraeus emphasized that airstrikes alone will not bring about regime change in Iran, pointing out that a sustained ground presence would be necessary to degrade Iran's military capabilities and disrupt leadership. He also stressed that the regime would not collapse solely from popular sentiment, contrasting the situation with the 'color revolutions' in Eastern Europe. Petraeus questioned whether US and Israeli air campaign

item.title: ShouXin’s Minimalist Cat Drawings Capture Motion and Personality
item.description: Artist ShouXin creates captivating graphite drawings of cats that convey both elegance and chaos with minimal lines. Rather than meticulously detailing every feature, ShouXin focuses on movement and expression, using a few confident strokes to depict a cat mid-pounce, mid-stretch, or rolling across the floor. The faces are rendered with care, especially the eyes, while the bodies are suggested with dynamic lines that give a sense of liveliness. This minimalist approach evolved over time, as ShouXin moved away from overly busy sketches to embrace simplicity and focus on what matters most. The resulting drawings capture the natural softness, speed, and unpredictable energy of cats, making each illustration feel alive and unique. Fans of his work can explore more online, with options to purchase prints and books that showcase this distinctive style.

item.title: Highland Park installs community sirens to alert residents of ICE activity
item.description: In Los Angeles’ Highland Park neighborhood, local activists are installing loud, portable sirens to warn residents of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) operations. These small, fire engine-red sirens, roughly the size of a football, can be activated via a mobile app and can be heard up to half a mile away. The project aims to help residents, including undocumented immigrants and other community members, quickly get indoors and avoid potential detention. About 20 sirens have already been placed in homes and businesses, with activists fundraising to install more. Flyers explaining the sirens are posted in English and Spanish, instructing residents to seek shelter when the alarm sounds. The effort comes amid heightened concerns about ICE raids following several fatal shootings in Minnesota in January 2026, increasing fears of aggressive enforcement tactics. While the Department of Homeland Security called the initiative a “public nuisance,” activists argue it provides a safe,

item.title: Psychologist Shares Four Healthy Distractions to Manage Stress and Regulate Emotions
item.description: In a HuffPost podcast episode of 'Am I Doing It Wrong?', hosts Raj Punjabi-Johnson and Noah Michelson interview psychologist Ryan Martin, dean at the University of Wisconsin-Green Bay and author of 'Emotion Hacks'. Amid rising stress levels from uncontrollable external events, Martin emphasizes regulating emotional reactions to protect physical health and relationships. He highlights healthy distractions as effective tools to interrupt panic and doom spirals by shifting focus away from stressors. The four recommended strategies include: 1) Contacting a friend for laughter and connection to quickly boost mood; 2) Using a 'visual security blanket' like saved funny videos, pet reels, or favorite TV reruns for instant comfort; 3) Engaging in absorbing activities such as coloring to redirect mental energy, even for short periods; and 4) Spending time outdoors in nature while focusing on a specific task like bird-watching or plant identification to enhance benefits beyond passive exposure. M

item.title: Diplomatic Deal Enables Limited Evacuation of Australians from the Gulf Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: A delicate diplomatic agreement has been reached to allow some Australians stranded in the Gulf region to return home amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. More than 100,000 Australians, including travelers and expatriates, are in the Gulf, though not all wish to leave. The deal involves backchannel negotiations with Iran, facilitated by Oman and Qatar, which have allowed the UAE to open a limited flight corridor. The corridor enables special commercial flights to bypass conflict zones, with Iran agreeing not to target these flights. Only a small number of commercial flights have operated so far, and land or alternative transport remains limited due to congested borders and airport capacity constraints. The UAE, keen to maintain its reputation as a safe hub, has announced a 'safe corridor' permitting up to 48 flights per hour, though the arrangement may be temporary. This initiative highlights Gulf states' interest in avoiding direct conflict with Iran while ensuring evacuation

item.title: US and Israeli Military Actions Against Iran Violate International Law and Challenge the UN
item.description: On February 16, 2026, economist Jeffrey Sachs warned the UN Security Council that the United States was on the verge of violating the United Nations Charter. Days later, the US and Israel launched a military attack on Iran without Security Council authorization, breaching international law under Article 2(4) and ignoring Iran's right to self-defense under Article 51. The article emphasizes that the attacks reflect US ambitions for global hegemony rather than national security and Israel's pursuit of a Greater Israel. The UN Security Council largely failed to condemn the aggressors, with countries hosting US military bases aligning with American positions. Only nations not under US influence, such as Russia, China, and Somalia, highlighted the illegal nature of the attacks. The article condemns the deaths of Iranian officials and over 140 schoolchildren in Minab as war crimes. It criticizes Israel’s justification based on religious and historical references and highlights the broader pa

item.title: Trump Administration and Tech Giants Sign Nonbinding Pledge on Data Center Energy Costs Amid Skepticism
item.description: On March 4, 2026, President Donald Trump hosted representatives from major tech companies including Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI, xAI, Google/Alphabet, Oracle, and Amazon at the White House to sign a voluntary, nonbinding pledge aimed at shielding consumers from potential increases in electricity bills due to the massive energy demands of AI data centers. Trump emphasized that data centers need better public relations and claimed the agreement would ensure tech firms cover their own power needs—potentially even building onsite power plants—to prevent higher utility costs for Americans and possibly lower them in some communities. This move addresses growing bipartisan concerns and voter backlash, as polls show low support for nearby data centers, and states like Georgia have seen legislative efforts (some stalled by utility opposition) to shift costs away from ratepayers. However, electricity experts, including Ari Peskoe from Harvard Law School, dismiss the pledge as mostly theatrical PR wi

item.title: Grammarly Introduces AI-Powered Writing Feedback Modeled on Famous Authors
item.description: Grammarly, recently rebranded as Superhuman, has expanded its AI features to include an “Expert Review” tool that offers writing feedback modeled on both living and deceased authors, scientists, and scholars. Users can receive suggestions inspired by figures such as Stephen King, Neil deGrasse Tyson, William Zinsser, and Carl Sagan, even though these individuals have not given permission or participated in the process. The AI agents are trained on the authors' published works to provide style and content suggestions relevant to the user's text. While the feature aims to enhance writing by simulating expert critique, it raises ethical concerns about using the work of living and deceased individuals without consent. Academics, including Vanessa Heggie and C.E. Aubin, have criticized the tool for treating scholarship as extractable content and for reanimating deceased authors in a way that ignores their personhood. Questions remain about the legality and effectiveness of such AI-generated

item.title: A Thoughtful Guide to Beautiful and Sustainable Camping
item.description: This article presents a guide to camping that emphasizes intentionality, sustainability, and aesthetic enjoyment of the outdoors. Rather than focusing on overpacking or flashy gear, it encourages campers to invest in high-quality, durable equipment that enhances the experience without harming the environment. Key areas covered include shelter, sleep, lighting, cooking, hydration, seating, packing, layering, and comfort. Recommended gear includes the Snow Peak Amenity Dome M Tent for minimalist elegance, the Big Agnes Tiger Wall UL2 for ultralight backpacking, and the Therm-a-Rest NeoAir XLite NXT sleeping pad for comfort. The article also highlights essential items like the Barebones Living Forest Lantern, GSI Outdoors Glacier Cookset, LifeStraw Go Water Filter Bottle, NEMO Stargaze Reclining Camp Chair, Cotopaxi Allpa 28L Travel Pack, The North Face THERMOBALL™ Eco 2.0 Jacket, Rumpl Original Puffy Blanket, and a Moleskine Field Journal for documenting adventures. The overall philosoph

item.title: AI and 3D Printing Enable Design of High-Performance Refractory Alloys for Aerospace and Defense
item.description: Researchers at Arizona State University and UNSW Sydney are leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) and 3D printing to redesign refractory alloys, metals that maintain strength at extreme heat and stress, for aerospace and defense applications. Traditional refractory alloys, based on elements like tungsten, niobium, and molybdenum, were developed decades ago and optimized for casting or forging rather than additive manufacturing. When subjected to 3D printing, these materials often crack or warp due to rapid melting and solidification. To address this, the team uses reinforcement learning AI to explore thousands of alloy combinations, optimizing for high-temperature strength, oxidation resistance, manufacturability, cost, and weight. Promising alloys are then tested in the lab, with experimental results feeding back into the AI model to improve predictions. The approach enables faster development of alloys suitable for hypersonic engines, nuclear submarines, and other advanced systems,

item.title: European Leaders Push Back Against U.S. Requests Amid Iran Strikes
item.description: Amid recent U.S. strikes on Iran, tensions arose between the United States and several European countries when Donald Trump requested access to airbases in the UK and Spain. Both Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer and Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez declined the request, prompting a series of sharp exchanges with the former U.S. president. European leaders appear increasingly willing to assert their positions independently, signaling a shift toward more cautious engagement with American military operations. Analysts, including Sky's military expert Michael Clarke, suggest that this change reflects a broader trend in Europe to balance alliance obligations with national interests and sovereignty. The disagreement highlights ongoing challenges in transatlantic relations, where European nations must weigh support for U.S. initiatives against domestic political considerations and strategic autonomy. The situation underscores the complexities of modern diplomacy, especially in matters of d

item.title: Israel and Iran Escalate Military Strikes Across the Middle East
item.description: The conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has intensified, spreading across the Middle East and impacting both military and civilian areas. Israel has carried out multiple airstrikes against Iranian and Hezbollah targets in Tehran and Beirut, while Iran launched missile and drone attacks on Israel and US allies. Over 1,100 civilians in Iran have been reported killed, including more than 180 children, with thousands more wounded. The US submarine attack sank the Iranian warship IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka, resulting in at least 87 deaths and raising concerns among India regarding international waters and regional security. Evacuations from the Middle East are increasing, with tens of thousands of US citizens and other foreign nationals returning home as airlines gradually resume operations. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping lane, is effectively closed to Western ships, threatening oil and food supplies globally. NATO intercepted a missile targeting Turkey, while Gulf

item.title: Trump Signals Imminent Endorsement in Tight Texas Senate Runoff Between Cornyn and Paxton
item.description: President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social that he will soon endorse one candidate in the Republican primary runoff for the U.S. Senate seat in Texas, pitting incumbent Sen. John Cornyn against Attorney General Ken Paxton. The two advanced to a May 26 runoff after finishing neck-and-neck in the March 3 primary, with Cornyn at 42% and Paxton at 41%. Trump emphasized the high stakes for the November general election against Democrat James Talarico, stating the intra-party fight 'cannot be allowed to go on any longer' for the good of the Republican Party and the country. He plans to urge the non-endorsed candidate to immediately drop out to unify Republicans and focus on defeating the opponent, whom he described as 'easy to beat.' Trump praised both candidates' primary performances but insisted the runoff winner must run a 'PERFECT' campaign, citing his strong track record of endorsements in Texas. Cornyn's allies, including Senate Majority Leader John Thune, have lobbied for his su

item.title: US Submarine Torpedoes Iranian Frigate IRIS Dena in Indian Ocean, First Submarine Torpedo Sinking Since WWII
item.description: On March 4, 2026, a U.S. Navy attack submarine torpedoed and sank the Iranian Navy frigate IRIS Dena in international waters in the Indian Ocean, approximately 40 nautical miles off the southern coast of Sri Lanka. U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth confirmed the strike during a Pentagon press conference, describing it as targeting a vessel that 'thought it was safe in international waters' and noting it marked the first time a U.S. submarine had sunk an enemy combat ship with a torpedo since World War II (referencing actions like those of the USS Torsk in 1945). The Pentagon released video footage showing an explosion at the ship's stern followed by its sinking. The IRIS Dena, returning from an international naval conference in Visakhapatnam, India, in February where it participated in events hosted by the Indian Navy, had about 130-180 crew members aboard according to various reports. Sri Lankan authorities responded to a distress call received around 5:08 a.m., deploying naval b

item.title: Economists Warn of Economic Risks Amid U.S.-Israel Conflict with Iran
item.description: Economists are expressing serious concerns about the economic consequences of the ongoing U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, emphasizing that the impact could extend beyond immediate oil price fluctuations. MS NOW analyst Steve Rattner highlighted that while U.S. oil supply may not be directly disrupted, prices at the pump and for home energy could rise due to the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz. This could contribute to higher inflation, rising interest rates, and increased market uncertainty. Nobel laureate Paul Krugman pointed out that the war adds another layer of unpredictability to an economy already affected by Trump’s tariffs, anti-immigration policies, and the uncertainties surrounding AI. Krugman described the conflict as a war of whim with minimal planning, warning that prolonged engagement could exacerbate existing economic pressures. University of Michigan professor Justin Wolfers stressed the international dimension, noting that U.S. markets may feel less impac

item.title: Spain Refuses U.S. Request to Use Bases, PM Sanchez Opposes War with Iran
item.description: Spain has taken a firm stance against participating in military actions targeting Iran, despite pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump. Following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, Spain became one of the few European voices questioning the legality of the operation. When the U.S. requested the use of Spanish bases for military operations, Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez refused, prompting Trump to threaten a trade war against Spain. Sanchez addressed the nation, emphasizing 'no to the war,' clarifying that his position was not support for Iran but a call for all parties to stop fighting before further escalation. He referenced previous U.S. interventions in the Middle East, warning that another war could destabilize Europe and risk millions of lives. The Spanish stance has drawn support from some EU leaders, including French President Emmanuel Macron and Spanish Vice President of the European Commission Teresa Ribera, who criticized Trump's threats as harmful to global stability. Meanwhile

item.title: The Benefits and Experiences of Guided Cycling Tours Abroad
item.description: Guided cycling adventures abroad offer a slower, more immersive way to explore destinations compared to faster modes of travel like cars or buses. By pedaling at an unhurried pace, riders can notice intricate details such as changing soil colors, coastal mist, and the architecture of rural villages built around natural features like springs or hills. In places like Portugal, these tours allow close-up views of functional regional homes—whitewashed walls for cooling, hand-carved doors, and stone constructions using local materials—revealing how communities have adapted to their environment over time. Cyclists often access hidden gems, including tiny chapels, ancient stone bridges, and quiet coastal paths inaccessible to vehicles. The journey highlights nature's textures: gradual shifts from forests to mountains, scents of the sea and wild herbs, and signs of sustainable land use like harvested cork trees, migratory birds, and traditional irrigation channels. These experiences foster a d

item.title: Anthropic CEO Accuses OpenAI of Misleading Claims in Pentagon AI Contract Deal
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei sharply criticized OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman in an internal memo, calling OpenAI's public statements about its new Department of Defense (DoD) contract 'straight up lies.' The dispute stems from failed negotiations between Anthropic and the DoD, where Anthropic refused to grant unrestricted access to its AI technology without explicit assurances that it would not be used for domestic mass surveillance or autonomous lethal weapons. The DoD, referred to as the Department of War under the Trump administration, subsequently struck a deal with OpenAI instead. OpenAI claimed its contract includes protections against these uses, stating that such applications are not considered 'lawful' and were explicitly excluded. Amodei dismissed this as 'safety theater,' arguing that OpenAI prioritized appeasing its employees over genuine safeguards against potential abuses, and accused Altman of falsely portraying himself as a peacemaker. He noted that Anthropic's stance re

item.title: South West Water Pleads Guilty After 2024 Cryptosporidium Contamination Sickened Hundreds in Devon
item.description: South West Water Ltd has admitted in court that it supplied drinking water unfit for human consumption during a 2024 parasitic outbreak in Brixham, Devon. The company pleaded guilty at Exeter Magistrates' Court to an offense under Section 70(1) of the Water Industry Act 1991 following an investigation into the contamination of the local water supply with cryptosporidium, a parasite that causes gastrointestinal illness including severe diarrhea and vomiting. The outbreak occurred in May 2024 and affected hundreds of residents, with several people requiring hospital treatment. Authorities issued a 'boil water' notice to approximately 17,000 households and businesses, instructing residents not to drink tap water unless it had been boiled and cooled. For some households, the restrictions remained in place for up to eight weeks while the contamination was addressed. The case was brought by the Drinking Water Inspectorate (DWI), which argued that the incident was serious enough to warrant se

item.title: NASA Restructures Artemis Program Following Artemis II Launch Delays
item.description: In February 2026, NASA’s Artemis II mission faced multiple delays due to technical issues with the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket. A hydrogen leak was detected during the first wet dress rehearsal, and days later, a helium leak emerged in the exploration upper stage, critical for propellant pressurization. These problems echoed challenges from the Artemis I launch, which experienced repeated delays. In response, NASA announced major changes to the Artemis program. Artemis IV, originally scheduled for 2028, will now conduct a lunar landing, overlapping with Artemis V, while Artemis III will become a focused rehearsal mission in low Earth orbit. This mission will test spacecraft systems, docking procedures with lunar landers, new spacesuits, and other critical technologies without actually landing on the Moon. NASA will revert from the planned exploration upper stage to the proven interim cryogenic propulsion stage to reduce technical risk. The revised timeline aims to streamline launc

item.title: Pentagon Identifies Six U.S. Soldiers Killed in Iranian Attack in Kuwait
item.description: The Pentagon has identified six U.S. service members killed in a surprise Iranian attack on a tactical operation center at Kuwait’s Shuaiba port. The soldiers, all from the 103rd Sustainment Command of the Army Reserve in Iowa, were serving at a fortified facility when a projectile penetrated air defenses. Among the fallen were Sgt. Declan Coady, a 20-year-old Army IT specialist and Eagle Scout from Des Moines; Capt. Cody Khork, a 35-year-old Army Reserve officer from Lakeland, Florida; Sgt. 1st Class Nicole Amor, a 39-year-old mother nearing the end of her deployment from White Bear Lake, Minnesota; Sgt. 1st Class Noah Tietjens, a 42-year-old Army Reserve mechanic from Bellevue, Nebraska; Maj. Jeffrey O’Brien, 45, of Waukee, Iowa; and Chief Warrant Officer 3 Robert Marzan, 54, from Sacramento, California. The attack, occurring on the second day of U.S. and Israeli military operations against Iran, came without warning and caused significant grief among families and colleagues. Each so

item.title: House Oversight Committee Calls Pam Bondi to Testify on Epstein Documents
item.description: The U.S. House of Representatives' Oversight Committee has voted 24 to 19 to summon former Attorney General Pam Bondi to testify regarding the handling of documents related to convicted financier Jeffrey Epstein. The decision, supported by five Republicans, reflects growing frustration over the Justice Department's management of more than three million files, which include emails, videos, and photos tied to Epstein's activities. Bondi has defended the department's actions while accusing Democrats of politicizing the issue. The move was championed by Republican Representative Nancy Mace, who emphasized the public's demand for transparency. Earlier, the Justice Department stated that no 'client list' existed and claimed it had completed its review, but Congressional pressure led to the release of the full collection. Last month, former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton met with the committee to address their past connections to Epstein, denying wrongdoi

item.title: Keith Olbermann Faces Backlash for Derogatory Comment on Lou Holtz Following Coach's Death at 89
item.description: Legendary college football coach Lou Holtz, renowned for leading Notre Dame to an undefeated national championship in 1988 and earning the Presidential Medal of Freedom from former President Donald Trump, passed away at age 89 on March 4, 2026, in Orlando, Florida, surrounded by family. The news prompted tributes from prominent Republican figures, including senators like Tommy Tuberville, Todd Young, Tom Cotton, and Lindsey Graham, as well as governors and other officials. No prominent Democratic leaders with football ties, such as Tim Walz, Cory Booker, or others, publicly commented on his passing at the time. On the same day, former ESPN broadcaster Keith Olbermann posted on X, responding to a 2020 clip of Holtz criticizing Joe Biden's stance on abortion rights by calling Holtz a 'legendary scumbag.' This drew sharp criticism from users who accused Olbermann of lacking class, decency, and respect, with comments labeling him as grumpy, lonely, and in need of mental help. The incident

item.title: Research Reveals How Zebrafish Larval Behavior Sheds Light on Human Handedness
item.description: Handedness, whether right or left, is a familiar aspect of daily life that shapes how humans perform countless tasks, from writing to eating. Surprisingly, this behavioral asymmetry is not unique to humans; many animals, including primates, birds, and even blue whales, exhibit similar lateralized behaviors. Researchers have long sought to understand why such asymmetries exist and whether they confer survival advantages. Recent studies using larval zebrafish have provided valuable insights. These fish display motor asymmetry, consistently turning left or right when exposed to certain stimuli, such as the loss of light. Scientists discovered that approximately 60 neurons in the thalamus, a brain region conserved across vertebrates, are linked to this directional preference. Removing these neurons eliminated the asymmetry, suggesting a clear neural basis. Experiments with multiple fish species confirmed that motor asymmetry is widespread, except in blind cavefish, highlighting the role of

item.title: Second Suspect Arrested in Connection with Snowball Incident Injuring NYPD Officers in Washington Square Park
item.description: In late February 2026, a large organized snowball fight took place in New York City's Washington Square Park amid a major blizzard, promoted via social media. When NYPD officers arrived to manage the disorderly crowd, participants pelted them with snowballs, some reportedly packed with ice or rocks. This resulted in multiple officers being struck, with two requiring hospital treatment for head, face, and neck injuries. The Police Benevolent Association and NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch condemned the actions as a serious assault rather than innocent play, emphasizing the injuries and potential long-term effects on officers. Police released photos of suspects and made arrests: first, 27-year-old Gusmane Coulibaly, initially charged with felony assault on a police officer but later reduced to misdemeanors (obstructing government administration and harassment) due to lack of direct evidence linking him to the injuries; he had a prior recent arrest for attempted robbery. The second arrest

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang explains decision to limit future investments in OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: At the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media and Telecom conference, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced that the company's recent investments in OpenAI and Anthropic are likely to be its last in both firms. Huang cited the upcoming IPOs of these companies as the reason, explaining that once they go public, investment opportunities close. Analysts note, however, that Nvidia's continued revenue from selling AI chips to these firms may also reduce the need for further investment. The company finalized a $30 billion stake in OpenAI’s $110 billion funding round, far below the previously pledged $100 billion. Huang denied any conflict with OpenAI, describing concerns of bad blood as “nonsense.” Nvidia's relationship with Anthropic has faced additional complications, including Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei’s past public criticism and the recent U.S. government blacklist limiting federal use of Anthropic’s tech. Following these developments, OpenAI quickly secured a Pentagon deal, boosting its product

item.title: US Lawmakers Seek Investigation Into Risks of Side-Channel Surveillance on Modern Devices
item.description:
Two US lawmakers, Senator Ron Wyden and Representative Shontel Brown, are urging a federal investigation into the risk that modern computers, smartphones, and other devices could be monitored using a long-known espionage method based on unintended electronic emissions. The technique—historically codenamed TEMPEST by the National Security Agency and now broadly described as side-channel attacks—relies on analyzing electromagnetic signals, sound, or vibrations produced by electronic components to infer sensitive information such as keystrokes, cryptographic keys, or internal computer operations.
In a letter sent to the Government Accountability Office (GAO), the lawmakers requested a formal study evaluating how vulnerable consumer devices might be to these attacks and whether government action is needed to require stronger protections. They argue that while the US government takes extensive precautions to shield classified systems from these emissions—often using specially shielded faci

item.title: US Military Kills Iranian Official Allegedly Behind 2024 Assassination Plot Against Trump
item.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that a US military operation killed an Iranian official accused of orchestrating an assassination attempt on Donald Trump in 2024. The operation targeted the leader of the unit allegedly responsible for the plot, which Tehran denies. This comes after Trump faced two assassination attempts while running for the presidency, one of which involved a sniper bullet grazing his ear. In November 2024, the US Justice Department charged 51-year-old Farhad Shakeri, an Iranian linked to the Revolutionary Guard Corps, with directing a network to target Trump. Two New York residents, Carlisle Rivera and Jonathon Loadholt, were also charged for their alleged roles. The charges include murder-for-hire, conspiracy, money laundering, and terrorism-related offenses. While the Pentagon confirmed the official was on the target list, it emphasized that eliminating him was not the primary mission of US operations. Trump later remarked that he “got him before he got

item.title: Rijksmuseum Authenticates 1633 Rembrandt Painting 'Vision of Zacharias in the Temple' After 65 Years in Private Collection
item.description: Researchers at the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam have officially confirmed that the painting 'Vision of Zacharias in the Temple,' dated 1633, is an authentic early work by Rembrandt van Rijn. The artwork, which had been held in a private collection for the past 65 years, was previously questioned by scholars in the 1960s and largely excluded from Rembrandt's cataloged oeuvre. A comprehensive two-year investigation, prompted by the current owner submitting it for analysis, utilized advanced scientific methods including X-ray fluorescence scans, detailed pigment analysis, brushwork examination, and dendrochronology on the oak panel. These tests revealed that the materials, techniques, and even the wood's tree-ring dating align precisely with Rembrandt's early Amsterdam period around 1633. The painting captures a subtle biblical scene from the Gospel of Luke, showing the high priest Zacharias in the temple, stunned by a divine beam of light announcing that he and his elderly wife will have a s

item.title: Large VA Study Suggests GLP-1 Medications Like Ozempic May Reduce Risk of Multiple Substance Use Disorders and Related Harms
item.description: A new observational study analyzing electronic health records from over 600,000 U.S. veterans with type 2 diabetes, published in The BMJ, indicates that GLP-1 receptor agonist medications—such as semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy) and tirzepatide (Mounjaro, Zepbound)—are associated with significant reductions in addiction-related outcomes. These drugs, originally developed for diabetes and obesity, appear to dampen cravings by acting on brain reward pathways involving dopamine. Among patients with existing substance use disorders, those on GLP-1 drugs experienced 50% fewer substance-related deaths, 39% fewer overdoses, 26% fewer drug-related hospitalizations, and 25% fewer suicide attempts over three years, equating to about 12 fewer serious events per 1,000 people. For those without prior addiction, the drugs were linked to an 18% lower risk of developing alcohol use disorder, 25% lower for opioids, and around 20% lower for cocaine and nicotine dependence, preventing roughly 6-7 new diagno

item.title: White House Outlines Iran Military Objectives Without Addressing Support for Iranian People
item.description: In the wake of recent US military action against Iran, the White House has outlined its objectives, but made no mention of supporting the Iranian people or promoting regime change. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt clarified that the goals of the operation focus on destroying Iran's navy and ballistic missile capabilities, neutralizing regional threats to Americans, and preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Despite former President Donald Trump's past comments about regime change, the briefing avoided any promises of backing an uprising or providing a mechanism for freedom within Iran. Leavitt stated that 'freedom rests in their hands' without offering specifics on US involvement. Ground troops are not currently planned, and suggestions that Kurdish forces could be used to inspire internal resistance were firmly denied. Leavitt emphasized that 'victory' will be measured by US interests, determined by the president once the objectives are achieved. No timeline for the military

item.title: Sky News and Journalists Receive Top Honors at Royal Television Society Awards
item.description: Sky News has been recognized as News Channel of the Year for the ninth consecutive year at the Royal Television Society Awards. Yalda Hakim, host of the international news program The World With Yalda Hakim, was awarded Presenter of the Year for her extensive reporting from conflict zones such as Israel and Ukraine and interviews with global leaders. Other Sky News journalists also received accolades: Africa correspondent Yousra Elbagir was named Television Journalist of the Year for her reporting on the Sudanese conflict, including investigations into areas targeted during the war. Home news correspondent Mollie Malone earned the Emerging Talent of the Year award for her coverage of UK prison safety issues, the use of drones to smuggle contraband, and the government’s strategies on violence against women and girls. Additionally, John Sparks received the Digital Journalism award for his coverage with Ukrainian troops in regions affected by explosive-carrying drones. Sky News also had m

item.title: Hezbollah’s actions continue to threaten Lebanon’s stability amid regional tensions
item.description: In early 2026, Lebanon faces renewed instability following U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran and the assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Hezbollah, a Shiite paramilitary group and Iranian proxy based in Lebanon, retaliated by firing rockets into northern Israel, prompting Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, Beirut, and the Bekaa Valley. Although Hezbollah has been significantly weakened since October 2023 due to targeted Israeli strikes on its leadership, it still retains the capacity to provoke conflict and destabilize Lebanon. Attempts at disarmament under a 2024 U.S.-brokered ceasefire have faced setbacks, as Hezbollah refuses to fully disarm while Israel continues its military actions. The Lebanese government recently outlawed Hezbollah’s military activities, a first in the country’s history, but the move has not immediately curbed the group’s operations. The situation heightens sectarian tensions, particularly affecting Lebanon’s Shi

item.title: House Oversight Committee Expands Investigation, Summons Bill Gates and Leon Black to Testify on Epstein Ties
item.description: The U.S. House Oversight Committee has broadened its investigation into the associates of convicted sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein and his close associate Ghislaine Maxwell. The committee, chaired by Representative James Comer (R-Ky.), has summoned seven additional individuals to testify, including Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Apollo Global Management co-founder Leon Black, and former White House counsel Kathryn Ruemmler. Gates is scheduled for a transcribed interview on May 19 and has confirmed he will cooperate fully, emphasizing that he never witnessed or participated in Epstein’s criminal activities. Documents released by the Department of Justice suggest Gates had personal affairs at the time, but he asserts they did not involve Epstein’s victims. Leon Black, who will testify on May 13, faces allegations in a lawsuit of sexual assault against a minor connected to Epstein, though he previously hired Epstein for personal tax advice. Ruemmler, who resigned from Goldman Sachs due

item.title: Insurance Sector Doubts Effectiveness of Trump’s Plan for Gulf Shipping Security
item.description: US President Donald Trump proposed US-backed insurance coverage and naval escorts for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz in response to rising tensions with Iran. The move was intended to secure the safe passage of tankers and other maritime trade, particularly energy shipments that account for 20% of the world's oil supply. Despite this announcement on Truth Social, global shipping premiums have surged as underwriters reassess risk. Experts from Lloyd’s of London indicate that the president’s proposals lack clear details, and the presence of US naval escorts could potentially increase the risk to vessels by making them targets for Iranian attacks. The Joint War Committee has expanded its high-risk designation to include the entire Persian Gulf, reflecting a substantial increase in perceived danger. Recent attacks on tankers and oil infrastructure in the Gulf, combined with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, have caused oil and gas prices to spike, along with shipping costs

item.title: Key U.S. and Global Updates: Flood Risks, Legal Cases, and Unusual Rescues
item.description: CNN’s '5 Things PM' highlights several significant stories from March 4, 2026. Rising sea levels are now estimated to be one foot higher than previously thought, prompting concerns about the increasing impact on coastal areas and populations worldwide. In Georgia, Colin Gray, the father of a school shooter, was convicted, marking another case where parents have been held legally accountable for mass shootings, sending a cautionary message to gun owners. Political coverage includes a profile of Jack Schlossberg, JFK’s grandson, as he campaigns for Congress in New York, offering insight into his views on family legacy, political issues, and national landmarks like the Kennedy Center. Health news focuses on erectile dysfunction as a potential early warning for cardiovascular disease in otherwise healthy men, emphasizing the importance of monitoring heart health. Culinary exploration introduces audiences to Korean side dishes beyond the well-known kimchi, encouraging readers to discover di

item.title: Los Angeles Designates Iconic 'Brady Bunch' House as Historic-Cultural Landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles City Council has officially designated the Studio City home featured in the classic television show 'The Brady Bunch' as a historic-cultural monument, ensuring its preservation and cultural recognition. Built in 1959 by architect Harry M. Londelius, the midcentury ranch became famous as the exterior of the Brady family residence, symbolizing the idealized American suburban family life of the late 1960s and early 1970s. HGTV purchased the property in 2018 for $3.5 million and invested $1.9 million to meticulously recreate the show’s interiors in a renovation project documented in the four-part miniseries 'A Very Brady Renovation,' featuring the original cast alongside Drew and Jonathan Scott of 'Property Brothers.' The renovated home now spans over 5,000 square feet, with five bedrooms, five bathrooms, and a hidden second story. After HGTV resold the home at a loss in 2023, it was acquired by historic-home enthusiasts Tina Trahan and Chris Elbrecht. The couple opened the

item.title: Study Shows Valuing Unpaid Household and Care Work Narrows US Income Inequality, But the Equalizing Effect Has Weakened Since 1965
item.description: A recent study published in the March 2026 issue of the Journal of Public Economics examines how including the economic value of unpaid housework and child care alters our understanding of income inequality and living standards in the United States from 1965 to 2018. Researchers valued unpaid domestic work—such as cooking, cleaning, and caregiving—using the typical hourly wage for housekeepers and incorporated it into measures of 'extended income' and 'extended consumption.' The findings reveal that unpaid labor historically acted as a significant equalizer because households across income levels spent similar amounts of time on these tasks. For example, adding the value of extra unpaid hours in lower-income families (often with a stay-at-home spouse) substantially reduced apparent income gaps between high- and low-earning households. However, over the five decades, the time devoted to unpaid work declined sharply, primarily due to women reducing their hours from 37 to 24 per week as m

item.title: Aaron Spencer Wins Republican Primary for Lonoke County Sheriff Amid Pending Murder Trial
item.description: Aaron Spencer, who is facing second-degree murder charges, won the Republican primary for Lonoke County sheriff in Arkansas, receiving 53.5% of the vote. Longtime incumbent John Staley received 26.5%, and third candidate David Bufford received nearly 20%. The primary victory positions Spencer as the frontrunner for the November general election, despite his ongoing trial for the October 2024 fatal shooting of a man accused of sexually abusing his 13-year-old daughter. Spencer has admitted to the killing but pleaded not guilty, arguing he acted to protect his child. His campaign emphasizes restoring accountability and integrity to the sheriff's office. The case has sparked national debate over a parent’s legal limits in protecting their child, drawing both support and criticism from residents. Spencer, 37, served in the Army’s 82nd Airborne Division and has military and private-sector leadership experience, which he cites as preparation for the sheriff role. His trial was postponed earl

item.title: President Trump to Attend Dover Ceremony for Six Service Members Killed by Iranian Drone in Kuwait
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to attend the dignified transfer ceremony for six American service members killed in a deadly Iranian drone strike on a U.S. tactical operations center in a commercial port in Kuwait. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt announced the president's intention during a Wednesday briefing, emphasizing his desire to stand in solidarity with the grieving families. The service members, ranging in age from 20 to 42, were part of Operation Epic Fury, an ongoing U.S. military effort amid escalating tensions with Iran. The attack occurred on Sunday, marking a tragic loss in the broader U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict that has intensified in early 2026. U.S. Central Command identified some of the fallen as Army Reserve soldiers from the 103rd Sustainment Command in Des Moines, Iowa, including a college student from Iowa, a Minnesota mother weeks from returning home, a Nebraska mechanic on his third tour, and a Florida captain with a lifelong calling to serve. These in

item.title: White House Prioritizes Price Stability Amid Iran Conflict, Per Leavitt
item.description: In the midst of escalating tensions in the Iran-US-Israel conflict, White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt emphasized the administration's commitment to maintaining price stability for American consumers. Speaking during ongoing live coverage of the regional crisis, Leavitt highlighted efforts to mitigate economic disruptions caused by surging oil prices following military exchanges between the US, Israel, and Iran. The conflict has rattled global markets, with oil prices spiking due to fears of supply interruptions in the Middle East. Recently, cheap gasoline had provided some relief to households facing high grocery and other costs, but the latest developments threaten to reverse that trend. Leavitt's remarks underscore President Trump's focus on economic resilience, as the administration monitors inflation risks and prepares for potential fallout from an extended war. Wall Street analysts are bracing for broader impacts, including higher energy costs that could fuel inflation and

item.title: Study Shows Shared Experience in Trauma Teams Improves Patient Recovery Times Through Better Coordination
item.description: A new study conducted at UPMC Presbyterian, Pennsylvania's largest major trauma center in Pittsburgh, reveals that trauma resuscitation teams perform better and help patients recover faster when team members have prior shared work experience. Trauma teams are often assembled spontaneously in high-pressure emergency situations involving severe injuries like car crashes, falls, or gunshot wounds, which are leading causes of death, particularly among young people. The research draws on the concept of transactive memory systems (TMS), a behavioral science framework describing a team's collective knowledge of who excels at specific tasks and who to rely on for particular expertise. This shared understanding reduces hesitation, minimizes questions during critical moments, and enables smoother coordination among emergency physicians, surgeons, nurses, anesthesiologists, and respiratory therapists. Analyzing data from multiple trauma cases, the study found that higher levels of shared experien

item.title: James Talarico Wins Texas Democratic Senate Primary, Faces Challenge of Uniting Party
item.description: James Talarico, a 36-year-old Texas state representative and aspiring Presbyterian minister, secured the Democratic nomination for the U.S. Senate after a competitive primary against U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett. Talarico’s campaign focused on a ‘politics of love,’ promoting economic populism and unity rather than combative politics. His approach appealed to voters seeking authenticity and a hopeful message, drawing significant grassroots support and widespread attention from national media. Key moments in the campaign included Talarico’s viral speeches, a high-profile interview with Stephen Colbert, and controversies surrounding remarks allegedly about other candidates, which he denied. Despite a highly organized campaign, he faced challenges from racial divides within the Democratic electorate, with Crockett receiving strong backing from Black voters, particularly in Dallas County. Talarico now faces the task of unifying the party ahead of the general election in November, while Republ

item.title: US Announces Deeper Strikes on Iran as Conflict Expands Regionally with Retaliatory Attacks and Evacuations
item.description: The US and Israel continue intensive military operations against Iran on the fifth day of the conflict, which began with joint airstrikes on February 28 targeting Iranian leadership, nuclear sites, missile facilities, and military infrastructure. US officials, including the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, stated that operations will escalate with progressively deeper strikes into Iranian territory, describing the campaign as still in its early stages. A US submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, and NATO defenses intercepted an Iranian missile heading toward Turkey after passing through Iraq and Syria. Iran has launched retaliatory missile and drone attacks on multiple countries, including Qatar, the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Israel, and others, though the volume of such launches has significantly decreased—down 86% for ballistic missiles and 73% for drones since the first day. Over 1,000 people have been killed in Iran, according to a

item.title: X launches X Money beta with William Shatner auctioning invites for charity
item.description: X, the social media platform formerly known as Twitter, has started offering limited beta access to its new payments service, X Money. Instead of distributing invites traditionally, X owner Elon Musk partnered with actor William Shatner to auction off 42 beta invites. Participants could obtain an invite by donating $1,000 to Shatner's charity supporting children and veterans. The auction was conducted through Shatner's posts on X, and Musk reposted highlights to his own feed. Winners will have early access to the X Money interface, which includes tabs for Account, Rewards, and Activity, with features like sending and requesting money, deposits, and direct deposit options offering up to 6% APY. Funds are held by Cross River Bank and insured up to $250,000 per individual. X Money is part of Musk’s vision to expand X into an “everything app,” alongside X Chat, video, subscriptions, and other services. The service is licensed in over 40 U.S. states and will begin a limited external beta be

item.title: Iran Appeals to UN Following US and Israeli Military Strikes
item.description: On February 28, 2026, as military strikes reportedly targeted Tehran, the Iranian Foreign Ministry issued a statement condemning what it described as 'criminal aggression' by the United States and Israel. The ministry called on the United Nations Security Council to take urgent action, arguing that the attacks violated Article 2, Paragraph 4 of the UN Charter, which prohibits the use of force against sovereign nations. The ministry emphasized Iran's commitment to defending its territory while highlighting its prior efforts to avoid conflict through diplomacy. President Donald Trump characterized the attacks as the beginning of a large-scale operation aimed at overthrowing the Iranian government, intensifying international concern. UN Special Rapporteur Ben Saul condemned the actions on social media, stating that the strikes represented a clear breach of international law and urging governments worldwide to denounce the violations. The incident raises tensions in the Middle East, with p

item.title: Seth Meyers Criticizes Trump for Revealing Military Plans in Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 4, 2026, 'Late Night' host Seth Meyers criticized President Donald Trump for publicly disclosing U.S. military plans during the ongoing conflict in Iran. Speaking about Trump’s remarks to CNN, where the president said the military was 'knocking the crap out of them' and promised that 'the big wave hasn’t even happened,' Meyers warned that such statements could compromise operational security. The comedian emphasized that the commander in chief should be able to keep military strategies confidential and suggested that Trump's excitement over the conflict leads him to overshare sensitive information. Meyers even performed a humorous impression, imagining more unintentional reveals. The broader context involves a U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran, which has resulted in the death of Iranian leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of civilian casualties, and fatalities among American service members from an Iranian drone strike. The escalating war has also disrupted global markets,

item.title: TikTok Confirms It Will Not Implement End-to-End Encryption for Direct Messages Over Safety Concerns
item.description:
TikTok has decided not to introduce end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for direct messages on its platform, according to a report by the BBC. The company stated that implementing E2EE would make it more difficult to protect users, particularly younger audiences, because encrypted messages would prevent law enforcement and TikTok’s internal safety teams from accessing message content when necessary. With end-to-end encryption, only the sender and recipient can read the messages, a feature widely adopted by platforms such as Signal, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger for one-to-one chats and calls, Apple’s Messages, and Google Messages.
TikTok emphasized that this decision is deliberate and is intended to differentiate its approach from competitors. Instead of E2EE, TikTok uses standard encryption to secure direct messages, similar to the protections used by services like Gmail. The company clarified that only authorized employees can access user messages and only under specific circumstances, such

item.title: Changes in Federal Funding Threaten Permanent Housing Programs for Detroit’s Homeless
item.description: Detroit has seen a worsening homelessness crisis, with around 1,725 people homeless on a single night in 2024, including hundreds of families and over 2,500 children experiencing unstable housing. The city’s homelessness response relies heavily on the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development’s (HUD) Continuum of Care program, which historically allocated 85% to 90% of funding to permanent supportive housing. This model, known as Housing First, provides long-term rental assistance paired with voluntary support services, improving stability, health, and reducing chronic homelessness. In November 2025, HUD announced a shift in funding priorities toward transitional and short-term housing, emphasizing participation in services before or alongside housing placement. Critics argue this 'treatment first' approach undermines evidence showing that stable housing is the foundation for successful recovery and long-term outcomes. Under the proposed changes, Detroit’s permanent supportive h

item.title: Los Angeles City Council Proposes Tax Amnesty for Cannabis Businesses Owing $400 Million in Back Taxes
item.description: More than 500 of Los Angeles' approximately 700 licensed cannabis businesses collectively owe around $400 million in back taxes, penalties, and interest, with the total reaching $417 million by December according to city officials. This includes about $100 million in penalties and $35 million in interest. However, only roughly $150 million is realistically collectible due to statutes of limitations and defunct businesses. The Los Angeles City Council unanimously voted to direct the Office of Finance to draft an amnesty program allowing businesses to pay principal taxes owed within three years, forgiving late fees and interest. Projections indicate that if about half of eligible businesses participate, the city could collect approximately $30 million in back taxes while waiving around $25 million in penalties. Of the collected revenue, 20% would support the general fund and finance office, 40% would fund enforcement against illegal cannabis operations by the LAPD and city attorney, and

item.title: Congress’s Struggle to Limit Presidential War Powers Over Time
item.description: Article 1 of the U.S. Constitution grants Congress the authority to declare war, yet modern presidents frequently claim Article 2 allows them to act militarily without prior congressional approval. Over decades, Congress has often responded weakly to such actions, from interventions in Panama to Libya. A notable exception was the War Powers Resolution of 1973, passed in response to President Richard Nixon's expansion of the Vietnam War into Cambodia. Despite Nixon's veto, Congress overrode it with a two-thirds majority, asserting its constitutional power. The resolution established rules for presidential military action, including a 60-day limit on hostilities without congressional approval, reporting requirements, and circumstances under which presidents may act independently. Since then, presidents from both parties have largely respected the reporting requirements but rarely sought explicit approval, often exploiting the 60-to-90-day window. Authorizations for Use of Military Force,

item.title: Israeli F-35I Shoots Down Iranian Yak-130 Fighter Jet Over Tehran in Historic First Air-to-Air Kill
item.description: On March 4, 2026, the Israeli military announced that one of its F-35I 'Adir' stealth fighter jets successfully shot down an Iranian Air Force Yak-130 aircraft over the skies of Tehran. This event marks the first time an F-35 has downed a manned fighter jet in combat, as well as a rare instance of air-to-air engagement for the Israeli Air Force in recent decades. Maj. Gen. Tomer Bar, commander of the Israeli Air Force, described the shootdown as a historic milestone that demonstrates the strength and determination of Israeli pilots amid the ongoing conflict. The F-35I, Israel's customized variant of the U.S.-made F-35 Lightning II, has been in service with Israel since 2016. The downed aircraft, a Russian-made Yak-130, is primarily a two-seat advanced trainer and light attack jet that Iran began receiving in 2023. The incident occurred as broader Israeli and U.S. operations continued against Iranian targets following escalations in the region, including prior strikes on leadership and

item.title: Michelin-Starred Chef Jean Delport Shares Insights on Great British Menu, Michelin Pressures, Customer Habits, and British Cuisine
item.description: In a monthly Cheap Eats interview with Sky News, Michelin-starred chef Jean Delport of Restaurant Interlude in West Sussex discusses his experience competing on last year's Great British Menu. He describes the intense preparation, often practicing late into the night while managing his restaurant, calling it an all-consuming yet rewarding challenge. His strong performance on the show significantly boosted bookings and revitalized his team, putting the restaurant on the national map and attracting diners from across the UK. Delport reflects on the ongoing pressure of maintaining a Michelin star, describing it as both exhilarating and exhausting due to the constant need to uphold and exceed standards. He advises guests to adjust tips based on service quality and to voice complaints politely during the meal for immediate resolution, criticizing the habit of leaving without feedback as unhelpful. A major pet peeve is customers mislabeling personal dislikes as allergies, which restricts kit

item.title: Offshore Floating Data Centers Powered by Wind Energy Could Address AI Energy Challenges
item.description: With the growing energy demands of AI data centers, some innovators have considered placing servers in space to access continuous solar power. However, a startup called Aikido is pursuing a more practical approach by situating data centers offshore. This year, Aikido plans to deploy a 100-kilowatt demonstration data center submerged within the floating pods of an offshore wind turbine off the coast of Norway. If successful, a larger-scale project is expected by 2028 near the UK, featuring a 15–18 megawatt turbine powering a 10–12 megawatt data center. Offshore placement provides consistent wind power, proximity to energy generation, and simplified cooling through cold seawater. It also mitigates local opposition from communities concerned about noise and pollution. Nevertheless, challenges include the corrosive nature of seawater and the need for secure, stationary placement of submerged equipment. While not a new idea—Microsoft conducted a submerged data center experiment off Scotland

item.title: n8n: Open-Source Workflow Automation Platform with AI and 400+ Integrations
item.description: n8n is a versatile workflow automation platform designed for technical teams that need both flexibility and speed. Combining a visual interface with the option to write custom JavaScript or Python code, it allows users to automate complex tasks without losing full control over their data and deployments. The platform supports over 400 integrations, includes native AI capabilities, and enables the creation of AI-driven workflows using LangChain with personalized data and models. Users can self-host n8n under its fair-code license or opt for a cloud deployment. Enterprise features include advanced permissions, single sign-on (SSO), and air-gapped deployments, making it suitable for large organizations. With a strong community, n8n provides 900+ prebuilt workflow templates and extensive documentation to facilitate quick adoption. Installation is straightforward via Node.js with 'npx n8n' or through Docker. The project name, n8n, is derived from 'nodemation,' combining 'node' from Node.js

item.title: Researchers Study Encounters with Nonhuman Entities During DMT Experiences
item.description: A new research initiative is exploring the experiences of people who encounter nonhuman entities while under the influence of DMT, a powerful psychedelic. The project, called Eleusis, is launching a retreat on the Caribbean island of Bequia, combining therapeutic support with scientific study. Participants receive DMT through an IV infusion, which allows for controlled, extended sessions known as DMTx, providing a longer and more manageable psychedelic experience compared to traditional vaping or ayahuasca ceremonies. The research aims to investigate whether perceived encounters with highly intelligent, alien-like entities are purely hallucinatory or represent an untapped dimension of consciousness. While proponents like neurobiologist Andrew Gallimore argue that these entities could exist independently of the human mind, other experts, such as Robin Carhart-Harris and Susan Blackmore, suggest these experiences are the brain’s interpretation of highly complex sensory input. Eleusis com
